status.comment: Hardkernel commits supply until at least 2036; product still listed in store as of 2026-04-26.

open-source-hardware.comment: Hardkernel publishes schematics PDF but does not release PCB design files under an OSHW-compatible license.

country-of-origin.comment: Hardkernel is headquartered in South Korea.

soc.comment: RESEARCH.md candidate label says 'RK3566S' but vendor docs identify the SoC as plain RK3566; Rockchip does not publish a 'RK3566S' part.

process-node.comment: Rockchip does not officially disclose the RK3566 fabrication node.

vulkan-support.comment: RK3566 GPU supports Vulkan 1.1 per Rockchip; mainline Linux uses open-source Panfrost which is OpenGL ES focused, so Vulkan availability in practice depends on driver stack.

npu-performance-tops.comment: Hardkernel quotes 0.8 TOPS @ INT8; Rockchip's RK3566 datasheet lists 1 TOPS as the SoC ceiling.

hw-video-decode.comment: RK3566 decodes H.264/H.265/VP9 at up to 4K@60. No AV1 hardware decode.

wifi.comment: No onboard Wi-Fi; Hardkernel sells an optional USB Wi-Fi/BT module (5BK).

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth; only available via optional USB module.

usb-a-2-ports.comment: 1× USB 2.0 Type-A. There is also 1× USB 2.0 micro-OTG, not counted here.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: USB-C port is power-only (5V/3A input); no data on USB-C.

mipi-dsi.comment: 30-pin 4-lane MIPI DSI; pairs with ODROID-Vu5S/Vu8S touchscreens.

mipi-csi.comment: No MIPI-CSI camera connector. The original M1 had one; the M1S omits it. (Note: the CNX-Software launch article from 2023-11-14 erroneously listed a 2-lane CSI connector; later vendor materials and the follow-up review confirm there is none.)

audio-jack.comment: No 3.5 mm jack on the PCB; analog audio signals are routed through the 14-pin J3 header instead.

gpio-pins.comment: Main 40-pin J4 header (mostly RPi-compatible). Board also has a separate 14-pin J3 header for ODROID accessories; not counted here.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 40-pin J4 header is described by Hardkernel/CNX as 'mostly compatible' with Raspberry Pi; some pin functions differ.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: PCB is 90×65 mm with a different mounting-hole layout from the Pi standard, so HATs do not fit mechanically; no HAT EEPROM.

idle-power-w.comment: Headless idle ~1.1 W; ~1.5 W with HDMI display attached. Vendor-measured.

cooling-required.comment: Ships with a passive heatsink; sustained ~3.5 W load is comfortable for passive cooling.

form-factor-class.comment: 90×65 mm — close to but larger than Pi-standard (~85×56 mm), and mounting holes do not match the Pi pattern.

weight-g.comment: Vendor figure includes the bundled heatsink.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: rk3566-odroid-m1s.dts is in mainline; Armbian and Gentoo confirm 6.1+ kernels boot with major peripherals (Ethernet, USB, HDMI, eMMC, GPIO) functional.

official-os-image.comment: Hardkernel publishes Ubuntu 20.04 (Linux 5.10), Ubuntu 22.04 (Linux 6.1), and Android 11 images.

distros-with-images.comment: Ubuntu via Hardkernel; Armbian publishes a board image. Manjaro ARM and others run via the ODROID community kernel but do not all publish a board-specific image.

long-term-support.comment: Hardkernel publicly commits to supplying the board until at least 2036 (~12+ years from launch).

msrp-base-usd.comment: Hardkernel store price for the 4GB base model on 2026-04-26. Launch price was $49 (Nov 2023); current pricing reflects RAM/component cost increases.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Hardkernel store price for the 8GB model on 2026-04-26. Launch top SKU was $62 (8GB + headers); current price is substantially higher.

available-regions.comment: Hardkernel sells with US/EU/UK/KR/AU plug variants; ameriDroid distributes in NA, ODROID.nl in EU.

typical-lead-time.comment: In stock at Hardkernel store as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive ODROID Wiki with schematics PDF, datasheet links, getting-started, and application notes; falls short of 5 because PCB design files are not open.

community-size.comment: Established Hardkernel ODROID community — large compared to niche vendors but smaller than RPi/Orange Pi.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum.odroid.com has a dedicated ODROID-M1/M1S subforum with regular activity.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Vendor-published cases, heatsinks, Vu5S/Vu8S touchscreens, UPS Kit, USB Wi-Fi/BT module, M.2 NVMe options. Smaller than the RPi ecosystem but well-curated.
